prompt_text: Help Scout vs Zendesk pricing and features
response:
Help Scout is generally **cheaper and simpler**, while Zendesk is **more expensive but more feature-rich**, especially for larger or enterprise support teams.[2][4] If you want straightforward email/chat support with lower entry cost, Help Scout is usually the better fit; if you need advanced routing, deeper reporting, phone/SMS, and broader automation, Zendesk is stronger.[2][4]

| Category | Help Scout | Zendesk |
|---|---|---|
| **Starting price** | Free plan; paid plans start at **$25/user/month** or **$25/month** depending on the plan structure shown in current comparisons[1][2][4] | Support-only starts at **$19/agent/month**; omnichannel Suite plans start at **$55/agent/month**[2][3] |
| **Higher tiers** | **Plus** and **Pro** are still below Zendesk’s Suite tiers in the cited comparisons[1][2] | **Suite Professional** at **$115/agent/month** and **Suite Enterprise** at **$169/agent/month**[1][2][3] |
| **Channels** | Email, chat, help center, social; phone/SMS typically via integrations[2][4] | Email, chat, help center, social, plus native **phone**, **SMS**, and **community forums** on higher plans[2][4] |
| **Automation** | Solid basic workflows and AI features, but less advanced overall[1][4] | More advanced triggers, macros, SLAs, skill-based routing, and enterprise automation[2][4] |
| **Reporting** | Standard dashboards; less advanced reporting than Zendesk[3] | Stronger real-time reporting and advanced analytics[3] |
| **Integrations** | Smaller marketplace, around 100+ in one comparison[4] | Much larger marketplace, over 1,500 to 2,000+ integrations in cited comparisons[1][4] |

A few pricing details differ across sources, which suggests either plan updates or billing differences: Help Scout’s paid plans are reported as **$25/$45/$75** in one comparison and **$22/$44/$65** in Zendesk’s own comparison page, while Zendesk’s Suite plans are consistently shown starting at **$55/agent/month** for the all-in-one package.[1][2][3] For the cleanest apples-to-apples comparison, focus on the fact that Help Scout’s entry price is lower and Zendesk’s feature set is broader.[2][4]

Choose **Help Scout** if you care most about:
- Lower cost
- Simpler setup
- Friendly interface
- Core support workflows for SMBs[2][4]

Choose **Zendesk** if you care most about:
- Advanced routing and automation
- Multi-channel support at scale
- Native phone/SMS/community support
- Stronger analytics and enterprise controls[2][3][4]

If you want, I can also give you a **feature-by-feature matrix** for Help Scout vs Zendesk, or recommend which one fits your team size and budget.
